metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Scope section to name `pyproject.toml` (the `[tool.mutmut]` section) as the file receiving `only_mutate` additions, and clarify that `docs/mutation-testing.md` receives only the recorded scores and test-selection mapping narrative.
findings.finding: The Scope section asserts that `only_mutate` additions belong in `docs/mutation-testing.md`, but the actual `only_mutate` configuration key lives in `pyproject.toml` under `[tool.mutmut]` — the doc only records scope and scores narratively. Any implementer following the plan's Scope literally will edit the wrong file and leave pyproject.toml unchanged, meaning the new shards will never actually run.
findings.suggested_fix: Change Scope bullet to: '`pyproject.toml` (`[tool.mutmut]` `only_mutate` list + `pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection` additions); `docs/mutation-testing.md` (shard scope documentation + recorded mapped scores)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `pyproject.toml` (the `[tool.mutmut]` `only_mutate` list and `pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection`) to the Scope section as a required edit target alongside `docs/mutation-testing.md`.
findings.finding: pyproject.toml is an unenumerated required edit target: the [tool.mutmut] only_mutate list and pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection live in pyproject.toml (lines 388-418), not in docs/mutation-testing.md. The Scope section lists only 'docs/mutation-testing.md (shard config / only_mutate additions)' — omitting pyproject.toml means an implementer following the Scope literally will not know to update the authoritative mutmut config.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope bullet to: '`pyproject.toml` (`[tool.mutmut]` `only_mutate` and `pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection` additions) and `docs/mutation-testing.md` (shard config narrative / recorded scores)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `pyproject.toml` to the Scope section alongside `docs/mutation-testing.md`, explicitly naming both `only_mutate` list entries AND `pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection` entries as required deliverables for each new shard (mirroring the existing paired-entry pattern).
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only `docs/mutation-testing.md` as the artifact for 'shard config / `only_mutate` additions', but the live shard mechanism requires updating TWO files: `pyproject.toml` (`only_mutate` list AND `pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection`) AND `docs/mutation-testing.md`. The existing pattern is explicit: every shard needs a paired entry in both. An implementer following the scope section literally could update the doc while missing the `pyproject.toml` config, producing shards that either don't mutate the right files or run against the wrong (full) test suite — defeating the stated goal of fast, focused mutation runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the Scope entry to: '`pyproject.toml` (`[tool.mutmut]` — `only_mutate` list and `pytest_add_cli_args_test_selection` entries) and `docs/mutation-testing.md` (shard table and score records)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a minimum kill-rate threshold to AC3 (e.g., '≥80% kill rate per shard, or document and justify any shard below that threshold') so the outcome is measurable, not just the act of recording.
findings.finding: AC3 requires mutation scores be 'recorded' in docs/mutation-testing.md but states no pass threshold, leaving the core claim — that new behavioral tests actually kill mutants — unmeasurable. A 0% kill rate would satisfy the AC as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'Mapped scores for the new shards are recorded in docs/mutation-testing.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC3 with: 'Each new mutation shard achieves ≥80% kill rate (or the score and a documented justification are recorded in docs/mutation-testing.md for any shard below that threshold); scores are produced by running `make mutation` (or the equivalent shard command documented in docs/mutation-testing.md).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a default rule for when extraction is required (e.g., 'extract a helper if and only if the decision logic is co-located with subprocess calls or I/O in the same function') so the executor can proceed without stopping.
findings.finding: The Scope section states helper extraction will occur 'if needed for tractable mutation' without stating a default decision rule, leaving the executor to determine whether extraction is needed — a real design choice deferred as a conditional placeholder.
findings.location: Scope — 'Small helper extractions in src/rebar/_store/{sync,push,lock}.py and src/rebar/_commands/compact.py decision logic, if needed for tractable mutation'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope bullet: 'Extract a pure helper if the decision logic under test shares a function with subprocess or I/O calls; otherwise mutate the existing function directly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the Scope file list with AC1's conditional descoping: either (a) remove `sync.py` and `lock.py` from the Scope list and note they are only touched if behavioral tests are added, or (b) make AC1's condition a positive gating statement with a clear decision rule the implementer can evaluate before starting.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists `src/rebar/_store/sync.py` and `src/rebar/_store/lock.py` as in-scope files, but AC1 explicitly descopes sync branch selection and lock stale-owner/reclamation unless this story also adds their behavioral tests. An implementer reading Scope first would expect to touch those files; reading AC1 they may conclude those files are out of scope — a genuine cross-section contradiction.
findings.location: Scope section (file list) vs. Acceptance Criteria AC1
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope section, change the file list to: `src/rebar/_store/{push}.py` and `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` (unconditionally in scope), and add a note: '`sync.py` and `lock.py` are only touched if this story adds behavioral tests for those paths; otherwise they are out of scope per AC1.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a minimum acceptable mutation kill-rate threshold (e.g., ≥ 80%) to the 'Mapped scores recorded' AC so the observable proof faithfully demonstrates that behavioral tests from S1/S3/S4 actually discriminate mutants.
findings.finding: The end-state proof for the plan's central goal — 'confirm the new assertions actually kill mutants rather than just raising line coverage' — is only 'Mapped scores … are recorded in docs/mutation-testing.md.' Recording a score of any value satisfies the AC, including a low kill rate that would indicate the behavioral tests are ineffective. No minimum acceptable mutation score is specified, so the observable proof does not faithfully demonstrate the headline intent.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Mapped scores for the new shards are recorded in docs/mutation-testing.md'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the scores AC with: 'Mapped scores for the new shards are recorded in docs/mutation-testing.md and each shard achieves ≥ 80% mutation kill rate; any shard below threshold must include a documented explanation of surviving equivalent mutants.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the opaque 'remote-append (S4)' label in AC1 with the concrete module path the S4 shard will target (e.g. `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` or the name of the pure helper to be extracted).
findings.finding: The AC references 'remote-append (S4)' as a mutation shard target path, but no module named 'remote-append' or 'remote_append' exists in the codebase. The closest candidates are src/rebar/_store/event_append.py (the canonical store write path) or a helper yet to be extracted, but neither is named in the plan. This makes AC1 non-verifiable as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC1: 'compaction fold/rollback (S1), remote-append (S4), push (S3)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
